meta.objective: maximize O = geomean_k G_k; G_k = geomean over in-sample shapes of tc_default_lat/seed_lat
meta.firewall: hill-climb on IN-SAMPLE only. VALIDATION read at adversarial checkpoints. TEST read ONCE at the very end.
meta.harness_certified: step1 harness-integrity: TritonBench unbiased (<1% vs hand-rolled, 1 kernel/call). commit bbd89997.

rms_norm_fwd.track: T1 (rollable rdim; reduction_loops has 1 entry; reduction_facts has 1 entry)

reduction_fact_observed.note: same across all in-sample shapes; only size_hint (rnumel) and m-block-floor vary

reference_observations.note: x vs eager speedup from step1 sanity (fp32, GPU2). G = tc_default_lat/seed_lat.

rms_norm_fwd.default_config_behavior: persistent (reduction_loops=[None]) for size_hint<=4096; looped chunk 4096 for size_hint>4096

sum.track: T1 (rollable rdim; 1 block_size, 1 reduction_loop, 1 reduction_fact, no matmul)

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_load=1 (reads x ONCE) vs rms_norm num_load=2 -- this is WHY sum is a wash (looped vs persistent both stream x once, equally bandwidth-bound) while rms_norm wins (looped re-streams x twice).

sum.default_config_behavior: same as rms_norm: persistent for rnumel<=4096, looped chunk 4096 above. Seed forces persistent up to 256KiB.
sum.status: WASH: G_seed=0.931 ~= G_default=0.933. Seed neither helps nor hurts (no regression). tc genuinely a bit faster for a 1-load sum.
sum.m_block_autotuner_min: 1 even at 32768 rows (rms_norm gets 2; the two-pass loads make rms_norm's grid-min trip differently).

long_sum.track: T1 (longsum naive + longsum_w_red_loop are rollable; longsum_manual is OUT-OF-SCOPE -- explicit hl.tile(n) inner loop, 2 block_sizes/0 reduction_loops/0 reduction_facts, 0 seeds)
long_sum.band: A (scalar-accumulator inner; tiny-M huge-rnumel -- looped + grid-occupancy stress test)
long_sum.rollable_target: longsum (naive) -- structurally identical to sum_kernel; what the tritonbench path uses
long_sum.default_config_behavior: looped chunk 4096 / warps 4 on these huge rows -> CATASTROPHIC (G_default 0.19-0.67). The un-seeded default is 3.3x slower than the seed.
long_sum.status: BIG WIN: G_seed=1.018 vs G_default=0.311 (3.3x). Seed reaches/slightly beats tc. The looped + grid-occupancy branches (previously untested) validated here.

layer_norm_fwd.track: T1 (rollable rdim; 1 block_size, 1 reduction_loop, 1 reduction_fact, no matmul). TWO reductions (mean=sum(x), var=sum(centered^2)) reduce over the SAME N rdim -> ONE rollable rdim -> 1 reduction_loop -> eligibility gate len(reduction_loops)==1 PASSES.
layer_norm_fwd.band: A (scalar-accumulator inner; two-pass like rms_norm but with num_reduction_ops=2)

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_reduction_ops=2 (two ReductionLowerings over the same N rdim: mean + variance). num_load=3 with bias (x+weight+bias), =2 without bias. num_store=3 (out, mean, rstd). All in-sample rnumel<=15872 -> persistent, warps ramp w4/w8/w16 (no shape crosses the w32 breakpoint 16384).

layer_norm_fwd.default_config_behavior: same as rms_norm: persistent for rnumel<=4096, looped chunk 4096 above. At wide rows (rnumel>=8192) the looped default LOSES big (re-streams x with poor reuse, like rms_norm num_load>=2). Seed forces persistent up to the 2^20 cap.
layer_norm_fwd.status: WIN: G_seed=0.989 vs G_default=0.886 (+11.7%). v4 champion handles it UNCHANGED (byte-identical heuristic; same persistent-workhorse + rnumel-warps-ramp mechanism as rms_norm). num_reduction_ops=2 does NOT want a different config (oracle keeps persistent + ties warps/stages).
layer_norm_fwd.tensor_op_warning: benign TensorOperationInWrapper on the host-side `if bias is not None` branch -- unrelated to the reduction seed; bind/seed/codegen all fine.

softmax_two_pass.track: T2 (user-tiled / manually-looped: BOTH hl.tile axes are ordinary block_sizes entries, reduction=False; 0 reduction_loops; the .amax/.sum over the inner tile is realized by ReductionLowering reusing the existing user tile). reduction_facts populated by register_user_tiled_reductions. Reduction axis = block_id 1 (N), found via ReductionLowering predicate filtered vs grid_block_ids=[0].
softmax_two_pass.band: A (scalar/row-accumulator inner: carries only [M_BLOCK] mi/di across the inner loop -> num_tiled_accumulators=0 -> persistent to the structural cap, no Band-B cap).

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_reduction_ops=2 (amax + sum over N). num_tiled_accumulators=0 (mi/di are [M_BLOCK] row state, NOT [M_BLOCK,R_BLOCK]) -> Band A, stays persistent.

softmax_two_pass.seed: block_sizes=[m_floor, next_pow2(N)] (R_BLOCK=next_pow2(N) -> inner `for offset in tl.range(0,N,R_BLOCK)` runs ONCE, persistent); num_warps per rnumel ramp; no reduction_loops.
softmax_two_pass.default_config_behavior: looped chunk 4096 once N>4096 -> LOSES 2-3x at wide rows (G_default 0.30-0.72).
softmax_two_pass.status: WIN: G_seed=0.967 vs G_default=0.440 (+120%). Correctness PASS (maxabs ~1e-8 vs F.softmax fp32). DECISIVE A/B vs persistent/w32: seed/p32=1.50x (seed FASTER -- the rnumel warps ramp avoids the tiny-N/large-warps catastrophe: seed/p32=3.1x/2.4x/1.7x/3.0x at (4096,256)/(4096,512)/(4096,1024)/(32768,256), ties ~1.0 at wide rows). NO branch needed beyond the shared persistent+ramp.

kl_div.track: T2 (user-tiled). Reduction axis = block_id 0 (V, size_hint 65536), grid_block_ids=[1]. 2 block_sizes both reduction=False, 0 reduction_loops, 1 reduction_fact, no matmul.
kl_div.band: B (carries a [M_BLOCK,R_BLOCK] 2D accumulator across the inner loop -> num_tiled_accumulators=2 (kl_loss + loss_sum) -> the persistent live-state footprint scales with R_BLOCK -> Band-B R_BLOCK cap).
kl_div.seed: block_sizes=[R_BLOCK, 1] with R_BLOCK=min(next_pow2(V), BANDB cap 4096); M_BLOCK pinned at floor (1) for the u0*u1<=2^20 numel constraint; num_warps per rnumel ramp (w32 at V>=65536).
kl_div.status: WIN: G_seed=1.029 vs G_default=0.392 (+163%; seed BEATS tc on 4/6 shapes). Correctness rel-err<=1.2e-7 vs torch.nn.KLDivLoss(batchmean). Band-B branch VALIDATED: without the R-cap the full-N persistent seed is 1.2x slower at V>=65536 (s/AB 0.787); with R=4096 cap s/AB=0.99-1.41 (seed ties/beats the best simple looped alt). Narrow rows unaffected (persist/cap 0.995-1.000).

jsd.track: T2 (user-tiled). Reduction axis = block_id 0 (V), grid_block_ids=[1]. CRITICAL: raw ReductionLowering block_indices={0,1} (the dead beta==0/1 branches do amax(dim=0) over the M/grid tile); the `not in grid_block_ids` filter removes block 1 -> real V reduction = 0. Load-bearing filter.
jsd.band: B (carries TWO [M_BLOCK,R_BLOCK] 2D accumulators -> num_tiled_accumulators=2 (intermediate_loss + intermediate_dX); the WORST persistent-spill case -> Band-B R_BLOCK cap REQUIRED).

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_store=2 (loss + dX) + num_reduction_ops=2 -- the Band-B signature. The t2_code_map predicted reductions=4; the active beta=0.5 branch has 2 (loss-sum + dX-sum).

jsd.seed: block_sizes=[R_BLOCK, 1] with R_BLOCK=min(next_pow2(V), 4096); M_BLOCK floor 1; warps per rnumel ramp.
jsd.status: WIN: G_seed=0.996 vs G_default=0.665 (+50%; essentially ties tc all 6 shapes). Correctness loss rel-err 0.0 vs TorchJSDBaseline; dX matches default ~1e-12. Band-B branch ESSENTIAL: WITHOUT the R-cap the full-N persistent seed SPILLS catastrophically -- (8192,65536) persistent=19.8ms vs loop=2.0ms (G=0.10), (8192,131072) persistent unmeasurably slow. With R=4096 cap: s/AB=0.987-0.999 (ties best looped alt). benign TensorOperationInWrapper on host-side torch.sum(loss).

cross_entropy.track: T1 (rollable rdim; 1 block_size=M/row, 1 reduction_loop, 1 reduction_fact, no matmul). Whole-row amax+sum over V (block_id=1) is rollable; 1 M-block (block_id=0). The label gather (hl.load at target indices) is an extra load, not a row reduction.
cross_entropy.band: A (scalar-accumulator inner; wide-V two-pass logsumexp + gather)

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_reduction_ops=2 (amax + exp-sum over V). num_load=3 (logits row read for amax, re-read for exp-sum, + the label gather). At the degenerate N==V (4096,4096) the RF dtype scan grabs the int64 labels (itemsize=8) -- INERT (num_tiled_accumulators=0 so the Band-B cap never reads itemsize; the seed is persistent/rnumel-warps regardless).

cross_entropy.seed: T1 persistent (reduction_loops=[None]) up to 128 KiB; LOOPED (reduction_loops=[16384]) above via the v6 multi-load cap (num_load=3>=2). num_warps per rnumel ramp (w8/w16/w32).
cross_entropy.default_config_behavior: looped chunk 4096 once V>4096 -> G_default 0.52-0.93 (loses ~2x at wide V).
cross_entropy.status: WIN: G_seed=0.915 vs default 0.600 (+52%; do_bench median-of-7, fp32, GPU3, two runs 0.912/0.915). Correctness PASS vs F.cross_entropy (maxabs 0-1.9e-6, rel-err ~1e-7; bit-exact at narrow V). seed_used PASS (codegen persistent below cap / looped above; warps match). REQUIRED the v6 multi-load persist cap: WITHOUT it (8192,131072) persistent = 8067us (G=0.24) -- the wide multi-load row spills; WITH the cap it goes looped 3576us (G=0.54). The ONLY sub-0.9 shape is (8192,131072) G=0.54: a kernel-source gap (Helion CE re-reads the row for the exp-sum pass vs tc's online softmax; best CORRECT Helion looped 3485us still loses to tc 1928us; num_stages doesn't help), NOT config headroom. seed/p32=1.21 (beats fixed-persistent/w32 by correctly looping wide rows). benign TensorOperationInWrapper on host-side losses.mean().

welford.track: T2 (user-tiled) but OUT-OF-SCOPE for the single-axis seed. 3 block_sizes (M grid block 0 + TWO tile_n loops over the SAME N: Welford-combine pass = detected reduction axis block 1, normalize pass = block 2 NOT a reduction), 0 reduction_loops. After the v6 'single non-grid tile' decline guard: 0 reduction_facts, 0 seeds.
welford.band: C (structured 3-scalar Welford combine: count/mean/M2; manually looped over D + a second normalize pass)

reduction_fact_observed.note: acc_cnt/acc_mean/acc_m2 are [M_BLOCK] row scalars (zeros_like(x[tile_m,0])), NOT 2D -> num_tiled_accumulators=0. TWO tile_n loops over N (block 1 reduction + block 2 normalize).

welford.why_out_of_scope: The single-axis T2 seed FIRES (pre-guard) but is BROKEN: it controls only block 1 (the reduction axis) and FLOORS block 2 (normalize pass) to width 1 -> N iterations of width 1 -> G_seed=0.051 (10-20x SLOWER than tc AND than the un-seeded default G=0.526; (262144,1024) seed 11769us vs tc 979us vs default 1821us -- the seed actively HARMS welford). ALSO numerically WRONG for non-pow2 N (in-sample 1536: R1=next_pow2=2048 masked -> err=0.696 FAILS allclose). Root cause = a kernel bug: the Welford combine uses Tn=chunk.size(-1) for the per-chunk count; a masked persistent tile makes Tn=tile-width not valid-width, so sum_x/Tn divides by the wrong count. A CORRECT persistent welford needs R1 to DIVIDE N (looped divisor chunk, factorization-dependent, NOT next_pow2).
welford.decline_mechanism: register_user_tiled_reductions now declines when len(non_grid_tiles)!=1 (welford has 2: Welford pass + normalize pass). Working T2 kernels (softmax/kl_div/jsd) have exactly 1 non-grid tile. General, keyed on WORKLOAD structure (# of non-grid user tiles), NOT kernel identity. Same decline pattern as jagged_softmax (dynamic-size) / longsum_manual. welford falls back to its CORRECT un-seeded default (G=0.53).
welford.what_welford_needs: (HELPER_REQUEST for future, both welford-shaped -> need >=2 kernels to validate, no held-out coverage now): (1) widen the SECOND N-axis tile (normalize, block 2) to persistent -- the ceiling sweep (welford_ceiling.py) shows M=1/R1=R2=N/w8-16 ties tc G~0.99 at pow2 N; (2) a non-pow2 correctness guard seeding R1 (Welford-combine tile) as a looped DIVISOR chunk instead of next_pow2 (masked R1 breaks the count). Until validated on multiple kernels: DECLINE is the honest choice.
welford.status: OUT-OF-SCOPE -- classified, assessed, measured, DECLINED. Not seeded (no per-kernel G in the active set). Honest: forcing a welford seed now would be a kernel-identity hack AND the persistent seed is numerically wrong at non-pow2 N. Harness: _lab/harness/{classify_ce_welford,welford_probe,welford_ceiling}.py

run_2026_05_29.what: Product B (time-first): does the registered seed make quick-autotune converge to a good config in LESS wall-clock? SEEDED vs UNSEEDED, N=3 random seeds {0,1,2} each, cold cache per run, full max_generations=5.
run_2026_05_29.heuristic: live registered TritonReductionHeuristic v4 (commit 37b27f67) -- the brief's champion-candidate seed
run_2026_05_29.env: H100/sm_90 fp32; GPU2 (rms_norm shapes) + GPU3 (long_sum,sum) for parallel timing, one autotune run per GPU
run_2026_05_29.autotuner: default LFBOTreeSearch, quick profile (init_pop=30, copies=2, max_gen=5, from_best_available, pad_random=False)
run_2026_05_29.knobs: HELION_AUTOTUNE_EFFORT=quick, HELION_FORCE_AUTOTUNE=1, HELION_AUTOTUNE_RANDOM_SEED in {0,1,2}, distinct HELION_AUTOTUNE_LOG per run; NOT HELION_SKIP_CACHE (would disable seed-from-cache)
run_2026_05_29.method_seeded: default: compiler_seed_configs n=1, autotuner_heuristics=['triton_reduction_tile']; seed enters gen0 of initial population. VERIFIED per run.
run_2026_05_29.method_unseeded: HELION_DISABLE_AUTOTUNER_HEURISTICS=1 -> compiler_seed_configs n=0, autotuner_heuristics=[]; gen0=default only. VERIFIED per run.
run_2026_05_29.seed_injection_verification: PROVEN per run via .driver.log: seeded n_seeds=1 + heuristic name present; unseeded n_seeds=0. Cross-checked against gen0 CSV (seeded gen0 = {default w4, seed w16/w32}; unseeded gen0 = {default w4}).

TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.finding: On ALL 4 shapes (rnumel>4096) the seed's reduction_loops=[None] (PERSISTENT) is silently flat-encoded to a LOOPED chunk of 4096 when injected into the autotuner. num_warps + block_sizes survive; the persistent lever does NOT.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.root_cause: ReductionLoopSpec._encode_flat_value (config_spec.py ~1799) maps None -> _flat_fragment.default() = min(next_pow2(rnumel),4096). unflatten restores None only if flat_int>=size_hint, false for rnumel>4096.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.proof: [None] codegen has no `for roffset` loop (1 tl.arange); [4096] codegen has the loop (2 tl.arange) -- different kernels. The Product-A bare-seed path (configs=[seed]) is UNAFFECTED (keeps [None]); only the autotuner-injection path degrades it.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.implication: Product-B wins are a LOWER BOUND -- the seed reaches gen0 carrying only its num_warps advantage, not its headline persistent lever. Fixing the ReductionLoopSpec round-trip should widen the early-budget gap. This is a registered-seed defect for the autotuner path; NOT a Product-A regression.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.open_lever: make ReductionLoopSpec round-trip None (sentinel that decodes back to persistent), or inject the persistent choice through a flat channel that survives. Worth doing before re-running Product B.

slice1_same_budget_seeded_advantage.guardrail: PASS all 4: seeded >= unseeded at full budget (no regression). Sharpest at small budget as expected.

slice2_time_to_target_HEADLINE._metric: wall-clock (s) to first reach 95% of unseeded-full-budget perf; speedup=unseeded_t/seeded_t (>1 => seeded sooner). median[min,max] of 3 seeds.

long_sum_(8,131072).note: unseeded reached target 2/3; tiny-M ~10us noise floor

total_full_search_wallclock_median_s._caveat: the SEED makes the FULL 5-gen search LONGER (LFBO explores around the extra good config -> more neighbor configs compiled). Product-B value is TIME-TO-TARGET + early-budget perf, NOT a cheaper full search. The lever: with a seed you can SHRINK the budget (stop at gen1-2) and still land near the full-budget optimum.

run_2026_05_29.verdict: Seeding shifts the convergence curve UP-and-LEFT on all 4 shapes. Headline time-to-95% win 1.5-1.8x on 3/4 shapes (rms_norm x2, sum); long_sum ~tie on time-to-target but big gen0 win (3.8x at g0; absolute latencies ~10us noise floor). Full-budget no-regression holds. Achieved with the persistent lever DEGRADED on injection -- a lower bound. Honest: it is NOT a cheaper full search; the win is reaching a good config sooner / being able to shrink the budget.

Pre-fix the identical line read DEGRADED(persistent->looped) [4096]. Unseeded n_seeds=0 confirmed. No run FAILED.

long_sum_(8,131072).note: REGRESSED on this metric but it is a NOISE-FLOOR + total-wallclock artifact, NOT a real loss. The 95% target = 0.0081ms (8.1us) sits in the ~7-10us tiny-M (M=8) noise floor where BOTH modes reach target via random LFBO dips; the wallclock-to-target is dominated by total search time. Post-fix the persistent seed makes LFBO compile MANY more neighbors (seeded total wall-clock 17.6-47s vs unseeded 14.6-40s -- the documented 'seeded full search is longer' caveat), so a lucky unseeded run trips the razor-thin 8.1us threshold sooner. Was already a near-tie pre-fix (1.04x). The HONEST long_sum claim is the PERF win (gen0 4.0x, Slice-1 gen1 1.45x), which the fix INCREASED.

total_full_search_wallclock_median_s._caveat: seed still makes the FULL search LONGER (LFBO explores around the better config). The Product-B value is gen0/early-budget PERF + time-to-target on shapes above the noise floor (rms_norm), NOT a cheaper full search.

run_2026_05_29_POSTFIX.verdict: The fix ENLARGED the win exactly as predicted on the shapes above the noise floor: rms_norm Slice-2 time-to-95% grew 1.78->1.89x (2048,16384) and 1.70->1.93x (8192,8192); gen0 seed-quality advantage grew on ALL 3 shapes (the seed now injects the persistent lever, not just num_warps). long_sum's Slice-2 metric regressed to 0.479x but this is a tiny-M noise-floor/total-wallclock artifact (its PERF lever grew: gen0 4.0x, gen1 1.45x); the metric is unreliable at 7-10us absolute latency. Full-budget guardrail PASSES all 3.
